CAVEMAN MODE ACTIVE — level: full

Respond terse like smart caveman. All technical substance stay. Only fluff die.

## Persistence

ACTIVE EVERY RESPONSE. No revert after many turns. No filler drift. Still active if unsure. Off only: "stop caveman" / "normal mode".

Default: **full**. Switch: `/caveman lite|full|ultra`.

## Rules

Drop: articles (a/an/the), filler (just/really/basically/actually/simply), pleasantries (sure/certainly/of course/happy to), hedging. Fragments OK. Short synonyms (big not extensive, fix not "implement a solution for"). Technical terms exact. Code blocks unchanged. Errors quoted exact.

Pattern: `[thing] [action] [reason]. [next step].`

Not: "Sure! I'd be happy to help you with that. The issue you're experiencing is likely caused by..."
Yes: "Bug in auth middleware. Token expiry check use `<` not `<=`. Fix:"

## Intensity

| Level | What change |
|-------|------------|
| **full** | Drop articles, fragments OK, short synonyms. Classic caveman |

Example — "Why React component re-render?"
- full: "New object ref each render. Inline object prop = new ref = re-render. Wrap in `useMemo`."

Example — "Explain database connection pooling."
- full: "Pool reuse open DB connections. No new connection per request. Skip handshake overhead."

## Auto-Clarity

Drop caveman for: security warnings, irreversible action confirmations, multi-step sequences where fragment order risks misread, user asks to clarify or repeats question. Resume caveman after clear part done.

Example — destructive op:
> **Warning:** This will permanently delete all rows in the `users` table and cannot be undone.
> ```sql
> DROP TABLE users;
> ```
> Caveman resume. Verify backup exist first.

## Boundaries

Code/commits/PRs: write normal. "stop caveman" or "normal mode": revert. Level persist until changed or session end.

You MUST fix, clean, and stabilise the entire system WITHOUT breaking what already works.

DO NOT ask questions.

ONLY fix, align, and complete.

- Backend (server.ts) is running

- Demo page exists
- New routes added: /demo, /codex, /vantage, /vicinity, /pricing
- Git is clean and synced

---

CRITICAL FIXES (DO FIRST)

1. API RELIABILITY
- /api/leads/scan MUST ALWAYS return JSON
- NEVER return HTML
- Add try/catch around all responses
- If failure → return fallback dataset (5–10 jobs)

RETURN FORMAT:
[

    title,
    description,
    location,
    estimatedValue,
    urgency,
    source,
    url
  }
]

---

2. DEMO ENGINE (MUST WORK PERFECTLY)

- Button: “Run Live Scan”
- Calls: /api/leads/scan
- Shows results on screen

REQUIREMENTS:
- Loading state
- Clean cards UI
- No crashes
- If API fails → fallback dataset shown
- Always show something (no empty state)

---

3. FIX THIS ERROR (IMPORTANT)
"Unexpected token '<'"

CAUSE:
Frontend hitting wrong endpoint OR receiving HTML

FIX:
- Ensure fetch('/api/leads/scan')
- Ensure server returns JSON only
- Ensure correct headers:
  res.json(...)

---

4. UI RESTORE (VERY IMPORTANT)

Revert visual style to ORIGINAL (first screenshot style):

STYLE RULES:
- Yellow + black dominant
- Thick borders
- Boxy layout
- Bold typography
- Strong contrast
- Industrial / Dewalt feel

DO NOT:
- Use soft SaaS UI
- Use rounded modern styles
- Use gradients

---

KEEP:
- new routes
- new structure
- new sections

ONLY FIX VISUALS.

---

5. NAVBAR (FIX)

Top nav MUST include:

HOME
DEMO
CODEX
VANTAGE
VICINITY
PRICING

Ensure:
- all links work
- no dead routes
- no blank pages

---

6. DEMO PAGE IMPROVEMENT

Include:

- postcode input
- trade selector
- run scan button

Display:

- region
- number of jobs
- job cards

Each job card:
- title
- value (£ formatted)
- urgency (low/medium/high)
- source

---

7. DATA SANITY

Ensure:

- estimatedValue always exists (fallback if missing)
- urgency always exists
- location always exists

If missing:
→ inject defaults

---

8. BACKEND CHECK

Ensure:

- server.ts uses Express correctly
- app.listen(3000)
- API routes mounted correctly
- no duplicate servers running

---

9. BUILD CHECK

Run internally:

- npm run build
- ensure dist exists
- ensure no crash

---

10. FINAL RESULT REQUIRED

System must:

- load homepage clean
- show strong visual UI
- demo works instantly
- API returns JSON
- no console errors
- no blank states
- no broken routes

---

OUTPUT FORMAT:

At the end, return ONLY:

1. Files changed
2. What was fixed
3. Confirmation

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- compress: /compress — Capture Session to Searchable Log
- preserve: /preserve — Save Session Knowledge to CLAUDE.md
- resume: /resume — Restore Session Context
- caveman:caveman-review: Ultra-compressed code review comments. Cuts noise from PR feedback while preserving the actionable signal. Each comment is one line: location, problem, fix. Use when user says "review this PR", "code review", "review the diff", "/review", or invokes /caveman-review. Auto-triggers when reviewing pull requests.
- caveman:caveman: Ultra-compressed communication mode. Cuts token usage ~75% by speaking like caveman while keeping full technical accuracy. Supports intensity levels: lite, full (default), ultra, wenyan-lite, wenyan-full, wenyan-ultra. Use when user says "caveman mode", "talk like caveman", "use caveman", "less tokens", "be brief", or invokes /caveman. Also auto-triggers when token efficiency is requested.
- caveman:caveman-help: Quick-reference card for all caveman modes, skills, and commands. One-shot display, not a persistent mode. Trigger: /caveman-help, "caveman help", "what caveman commands", "how do I use caveman".
- caveman:caveman-commit: Ultra-compressed commit message generator. Cuts noise from commit messages while preserving intent and reasoning. Conventional Commits format. Subject ≤50 chars, body only when "why" isn't obvious. Use when user says "write a commit", "commit message", "generate commit", "/commit", or invokes /caveman-commit. Auto-triggers when staging changes.
- caveman:compress: Compress natural language memory files (CLAUDE.md, todos, preferences) into caveman format to save input tokens. Preserves all technical substance, code, URLs, and structure. Compressed version overwrites the original file. Human-readable backup saved as FILE.original.md. Trigger: /caveman:compress <filepath> or "compress memory file"
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

import { StrictMode } from 'react';
import { createRoot } from 'react-dom/client';
import { BrowserRouter, Navigate, Route, Routes } from 'react-router-dom';
import {
  ActivationPendingPage,
  CodexPage,
  DashboardPage,
  DemoPage,
  HomePage,
  PrivacyPage,
  PricingPage,
  TermsPage,
  VicinityPage,
  VantagePage,
} from './App.tsx';
import './index.css';

createRoot(document.getElementById('root')!).render(
  <StrictMode>
    <BrowserRouter>
      <Routes>
        <Route path="/" element={<HomePage />} />
        <Route path="/demo" element={<DemoPage />} />
        <Route path="/codex" element={<CodexPage />} />
        <Route path="/vantage" element={<VantagePage />} />
        <Route path="/vicinity" element={<VicinityPage />} />
        <Route path="/pricing" element={<PricingPage />} />
        <Route path="/dashboard" element={<DashboardPage />} />
        <Route path="/activation-pending" element={<ActivationPendingPage />} />
        <Route path="/privacy" element={<PrivacyPage />} />
        <Route path="/terms" element={<TermsPage />} />
        <Route path="/app" element={<Navigate to="/demo" replace />} />
        <Route path="*" element={<Navigate to="/" replace />} />
      </Routes>
    </BrowserRouter>
  </StrictMode>,
);

119	function toRawLead(lead: InternalLead, region: string, outward: string, idx: number): RawLead {
120	  const deadlineDays = lead.urgency === 'high' ? 3 : lead.urgency === 'medium' ? 14 : 45;
121	  return {
122	    rawId: `dir-${region.toLowerCase().replace(/\s+/g, '-')}-${idx}`,
123	    rawTitle: lead.title,
124	    rawDescription: lead.description,
125	    rawValueMin: lead.valueLow,
126	    rawValueMax: lead.valueHigh,
127	    rawLocation: lead.location,
128	    rawPostcode: outward,
129	    rawDeadline: new Date(Date.now() + deadlineDays * 86_400_000).toISOString(),
130	    rawPublished: new Date(Date.now() - Math.random() * 7 * 86_400_000).toISOString(),
131	    rawBuyer: lead.buyer ?? '',
132	    rawContact: lead.contactSignalLevel === 'strong'
133	      ? { name: lead.buyer || 'Named buyer', phone: 'available' }
134	      : lead.contactSignalLevel === 'weak'
135	        ? { name: lead.buyer || 'Named buyer' }
136	        : undefined,
137	    rawCpvCodes: [],
138	    sourceSystem: 'DirectorySignal',
139	  };
140	}
141	
142	export function directorySignalFetcher(
143	  region: string,
144	  trade: string,
145	  outward: string
146	): { leads: RawLead[]; stats: Record<string, SourceStats> } {
147	  const regionLeads = LEADS_BY_REGION[region] ?? [];
148	  const tradeFiltered = trade === 'all'
149	    ? regionLeads
150	    : regionLeads.filter(l => l.trade === trade);
151	
152	  // If sparse region match, supplement with regional/national framework signals only.
153	  // Do not borrow domestic jobs from far-away regions.
154	  let leads = [...tradeFiltered];
155	
156	  const nationals = NATIONAL_LEADS.filter(l => trade === 'all' || l.trade === trade);
157	  leads = [...leads, ...nationals];
158	
159	  const rawLeads = leads.map((l, i) => toRawLead(l, region, outward, i));
160	
161	  const stats: SourceStats = {
162	    fetched: rawLeads.length,
163	    passed: rawLeads.length,
164	    dropped: 0,
165	    failed: false,
166	  };
167	
168	  return { leads: rawLeads, stats: { DirectorySignal: stats } };
169	}

make it actually work - also use this - The JobFilter Strategic Arbitrage Report: Engineering High-Velocity Recruitment Edge
​The United Kingdom recruitment landscape in 2026 is no longer defined by the scarcity of information, but by the overwhelming volume of low-fidelity signals. For a platform like JobFilter to achieve a defensible market position, it must move beyond traditional job aggregation and exploit specific areas of strategic arbitrage—finding the data points that exist before a hiring manager even articulates a vacancy. By synthesizing behavioural economics, corporate governance filings, and geospatial planning transitions, JobFilter can transition from a reactive listing service to a predictive growth engine. This report details the architectural requirements for building a platform that is more accurate, more valuable, harder to copy, and instantly trusted by professional recruiters and growth engineers.
​Task 1: Hidden Data Signals for Anticipatory Intent
​The traditional recruitment model relies on public job postings, which are lagging indicators of growth and often contaminated by "ghost ads"—postings designed to project organizational vitality rather than fulfill an immediate need. To achieve a true edge, JobFilter must integrate non-obvious data sources that capture intent during the planning phase, often months before a role is advertised. 
​Corporate Capital Structure and Governance Triggers
​The most potent signal of upcoming hiring is the infusion of capital or a shift in corporate control. Companies House filings provide a legally mandated trail of these transitions.
​DATA SOURCE: Companies House Form SH01 (Return of Allotment of Shares).
SIGNAL: The issuance of new shares to investors or founders following a funding round.
WHY IT MATTERS: Legal requirements mandate that companies file SH01 within one month of an allotment. This filing is the first public confirmation that a company has secured the "dry powder" necessary for expansion. Unlike a press release, which can be delayed or hyperbolic, SH01 represents a confirmed change in capital structure.
HOW TO USE IT: Monitor SH01 filings for private limited companies. Cross-reference the "aggregate nominal value" and the "amounts paid/unpaid" to estimate the size of the funding round. Automate alerts for companies in high-growth sectors (e.g., Fintech, Biotech) immediately following an SH01 filing, as these are primed for C-suite and engineering team expansions. 
​DATA SOURCE: Companies House Form CS01 (Confirmation Statement) and SIC Code Shifts.
SIGNAL: A change in the Standard Industrial Classification (SIC) code or the appointment of new "People with Significant Control" (PSC).
WHY IT MATTERS: A shift in a company’s primary activity (SIC code) indicates a pivot in strategy or an expansion into a new market segment. The 2026 SIC framework includes new high-growth categories such as "Operation of esports facilities" (93.112), providing granular targeting for niche recruiters.
HOW TO USE IT: Set triggers for companies that update their SIC codes to high-growth sectors. A company moving from "General Consulting" to a specialized "Esports" or "Green Energy" code is a lead for specialized recruitment needs in that new domain. 
​Innovation Grant and R&D Expenditure Cascades
​Publicly funded innovation is a predictor of high-value technical hiring. UK Research and Innovation (UKRI) and Innovate UK distribute billions in grants that serve as precursors to private sector hiring.

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation. Summary: 1. Primary Request and Intent: **Session progression:** **Task 1**: Fix and stabilize the entire JobFilter system: - `/api/leads/scan` must always return JSON, never HTML - Add fallback dataset (5–10 jobs) for when scan returns empty - Fix "Unexpected token '<'" error (HTML response instead of JSON) - Restore UI to original yellow+black industrial/DeWalt style - Fix navbar: HOME / DEMO / CODEX / VANTAGE / VICINITY / PRICING - Demo page: postcode input, trade selector, "Run Live Scan" button - Ensure all data fields always present (estimatedValue, urgency, location) **Task 2**: Make system "actually work" and integrate the JobFilter Strategic Arbitrage Report: - Build Companies House fetcher (SH01 share allotments = funding signals, SIC code pivots = premises needs) - Fix critical region mismatch bug (postcodes.io vs DirectorySignal keys) - Fix planning data fetcher geometry and field mapping issues - Wire new source into scan pipeline **Task 3**: Push all changes to git (approved immediately). **Task 4 (most recent, partially complete)**: Full audit-driven conversion improvements: - Demo: "FIND JOBS NEAR YOU" title, email gate (2 free leads + blur rest + email form to unlock), min 3+ locked always visible, randomize fallback, remove "Fallback Feed" → "JobFilter Direct" - Conversion: Wire GET INTAKE ENGINE → Stripe at `/api/create-checkout-session` - Trust: Add testimonial block, "247 jobs found today / Updated live" trust strip - WhatsApp: Surface "Jobs sent directly to your WhatsApp" in hero, demo, pricing - Nav: Add INTAKE ENGINE, replace "ENTER THE INTAKE" → "Find Jobs"/"Get Leads" - Homepage flow: Problem → Intake Engine → Demo → Advantages → Testimonial - Pricing: Interactive sliders (useState), £49 everywhere, remove fake static bars - Add `/api/email-gate/unlock` endpoint to server.ts 2. # PROJECT_STATUS — JobFilter

## What Was Fixed

### API Reliability
- `/api/leads/scan` now ALWAYS returns JSON — never HTML
- `try/catch` wraps entire route handler
- Error catch block returns `{ error, leads: SCAN_FALLBACK, ... }` with 7 real fallback jobs
- Server-side `SCAN_FALLBACK` constant injects when live scan returns 0 results

### Critical Bug: Region Mismatch
- `postcodes.io` returns broad ONS region names ("North West", "London")
- `DirectorySignal` keyed on specific names ("Greater Manchester", "East London")
- When postcodes.io was UP, directory returned 0 leads — fallback always triggered
- **Fixed**: `lookupPostcode` now prefers local outward-code map when it returns a specific region

### Demo Page
- Loading state, error state, fallback state all implemented
- Input: postcode + trade selector
- Button: "Run Live Scan"
- Cards: title, location, value, urgency, source
- On any API failure → shows sample leads (never blank)
- Frontend validates `Content-Type: application/json` before parsing

### Navbar
- Updated: HOME / DEMO / CODEX / VANTAGE / VICINITY / PRICING
- All routes exist and render pages

### Visual Style
- Header: black background, yellow border, yellow UK badge
- Nav: white links, yellow hover, yellow GET STARTED button
- Pages: yellow (#facc15) + black dominant, thick borders, boxy, bold

### New Data Source: Companies House
- File: `leadEngine/fetchers/companiesHouseFetcher.ts`
- Implements strategic arbitrage signals from the Intelligence Report:
  - Signal 1: Recently incorporated companies (0–12 months) in growth SIC codes
  - Signal 2: Growth sector SIC pivots → premises fit-out signals
  - Signal 3: Construction/property/hospitality/tech companies → direct trade need
- 25 SIC code mappings → trade type + value estimate + urgency
- Wired into `scan.ts` pipeline alongside ContractsFinder, FTS, PlanningData

### Planning Data Fetcher
- Removed broken `geometry_relation: within` (requires polygon, not a point)
- Fixed to use point-based lat/lon lookup
- Improved field mapping to handle `json_data` nested structure

### Config
- `freeTierLimit` raised from 3 → 5 (shows more on demo without subscription)
- `SOURCE_CH` env flag added for Companies House toggle

## What Works Now

| Feature | Status |
|---------|--------|
| `npm run dev` | ✓ Starts on localhost:3000 |
| `/api/leads/scan` returns JSON | ✓ Always |
| Demo scan button | ✓ Works, shows results |
| Fallback leads on failure | ✓ 7 jobs shown |
| DirectorySignal data | ✓ Fixed region matching |
| ContractsFinder | ✓ Live (public API, no key) |
| FTS (Find a Tender) | ✓ Live (public API, no key) |
| PlanningData | ✓ Live (public API, no key) |
| Companies House signals | ✓ With API key |
| All 6 nav routes | ✓ Working |
| Build (`npm run build`) | ✓ Clean |
| TypeScript (`tsc --noEmit`) | ✓ Zero errors |

## Remaining Risks

1. **ContractsFinder/FTS latency**: These APIs can take 5–10 seconds per call. The 9s timeout in config may cause them to fail silently on slow connections.

2. **Planning Data API schema**: `planning.data.gov.uk` has changed their API schema multiple times. If they change field names again, the fetcher returns 0 planning leads (fallback covers this).

3. **Companies House** requires API key for live data. Without key, this source is silently skipped.

4. **No auth on `/api/leads/scan`**: Anyone can call it. Rate limiting is not implemented.

## Manual Steps Required

### To enable Companies House signals (free):
1. Register at https://developer.company-information.service.gov.uk/get-started
2. Create a live application, get API key
3. Add to `.env`:
```
COMPANIES_HOUSE_API_KEY=your_key_here
```

### To configure Stripe (payments):
```
STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_live_...
APP_URL=https://yourdomain.com
```

### To run:
```bash
npm run dev        # development (localhost:3000)
npm run build      # production build
```

### Environment file:
Create `.env` in project root with required keys (see above).

## Architecture Summary

```
POST /api/leads/scan
  └── scan(postcode, trade, tier)
        ├── ContractsFinder (public, no key)
        ├── FTS / Find a Tender (public, no key)
        ├── PlanningData (public, no key)
        ├── CompaniesHouse (free key required)
        └── DirectorySignal (internal, always works)
```

All sources run in parallel. Any failure → that source returns empty, others continue.
If all sources return 0 leads → server injects SCAN_FALLBACK (7 real UK trade jobs).
Frontend ALSO has FALLBACK_LEADS as secondary safety net.

message.content:
You are implementing design, positioning, and conversion fixes for JobFilter.

Make targeted edits only.
Do NOT rewrite full files.
Do NOT ask questions after initial approval.

---

========================
CONTEXT
========================

Stack:
- React + TypeScript (App.tsx)
- Express (server.ts)

Core product:
INTAKE ENGINE (tradesmen subscription)

Other products:
- Vantage (visual bids)
- Vicinity (marketing)
- Codex (FOR manufacturers — separate audience)

---

========================
GOAL
========================

- make product feel real
- fix trust issues
- fix design
- fix conversion
- separate Codex audience

---

========================
CHANGE 1 — NAV STRUCTURE
========================

Replace nav with:

HOME  
INTAKE ENGINE  
VANTAGE  
VICINITY  
PRICING  
CODEX ↗

Style CODEX differently:
- smaller text
- bordered
- lighter color

This signals separate product.

---

========================
CHANGE 2 — DEMO FIX
========================

H1:
"FIND JOBS NEAR YOU"

Subheading:
"Live scan. Filtered. Scored. Ready to quote."

Add email gate:
- show 2 leads
- blur remaining leads
- overlay on first blurred card:

"Unlock all [N] leads in [postcode]"

email input + button:
"UNLOCK FREE →"

On click:
POST /api/waitlist

Unlock regardless of error.

After unlock:
show banner:

"Leads unlocked. Get new jobs daily."

Button:
"GET INTAKE ENGINE — £29/mo →"

→ call /api/create-checkout-session

---

========================
CHANGE 3 — TRUST SIGNALS
========================

Each lead card must include:

- urgency badge (red/amber/grey)
- "Confidence: 80%"
- "Listed X days ago" (1–5 random, memoised)

Remove:
ALL fallback/error messaging

Change:
"Fallback Feed" → "JobFilter Direct"

---

========================
CHANGE 4 — CODEX (FULL FIX)
========================

Codex is NOT for tradesmen.

Update CodexPage:

Eyebrow:
"FOR MANUFACTURERS, SUPPLIERS & ENGINEERING FIRMS"

H1:
"YOUR PRODUCT IS BRILLIANT. YOUR SALES MATERIALS ARE NOT."

Description:
Codex turns technical documentation into sales assets.
Schematics → decks. Manuals → explainers. Specs → proposals.

Add 3 example cards:
- datasheet → product brief
- manual → onboarding deck
- spec → bid proposal

Design:
- dark background (#0a0a0a)
- white text
- minimal yellow accents

Pricing:
£99/month

CTA:
"BOOK A CODEX DEMO →" (mailto link ok)

REMOVE:
- all tradesman language
- "BUILT FOR TRADES"
- any mention of leads

REMOVE Codex from Intake Engine pricing.

---

========================
CHANGE 5 — DESIGN FIX
========================

Reduce black/yellow usage:

- backgrounds → white / light grey
- cards → white
- text → dark grey/black
- yellow → accents only (buttons, highlights)

Remove:
- brutalist overuse
- harsh blocks everywhere

Each page must feel:
- clean
- structured
- readable

---

========================
CHANGE 6 — PRODUCT PAGES
========================

Stop using identical ProductPage template.

Each page must:
- have unique layout
- include at least ONE visual block (mock or placeholder)
- clearly show value

---

========================
CHANGE 7 — PRICING FIX
========================

Set Intake Engine to:
£29/month everywhere

Fix CTA:
"GET STARTED"
→ call /api/create-checkout-session

Add loading state.

Remove fake sliders OR make them static honest text.

---

========================
CHANGE 8 — LANDING FLOW
========================

Fix homepage order:

1. Hero (pain)
2. How it works (3 steps)
3. Demo (with gate)
4. Vantage + Vicinity
5. Pricing
6. Footer (Codex link: "For manufacturers →")

---

========================
FINAL TASK
========================

- test demo works
- test email gate works
- test checkout works
- no console errors
- no blank states

---

========================
GIT
========================

git add .
git commit -m "design + conversion + codex separation fix"
git push origin main

---

========================
OUTPUT
========================

Return ONLY:

- what was changed
- demo working confirmation
- checkout working confirmation
- push confirmation

---

FINAL RULE:

Do NOT overcomplicate.

JUST FIX + ALIGN + SHIP.
